spec_match.rationale: Accurately matches the spec by contrasting three different fetch mechanisms and rendering their actual headers live in a Service Worker tracer.

interactive.rationale: Provides clear buttons to install the SW, trigger link prefetch, speculation prefetch, or raw fetch, rendering details of the captured headers dynamically.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns perfectly with the index page description, providing a compatibility matrix and dynamic header capturing.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers link prefetch, speculationrules prefetch, and plain fetch. Also mentions speculationrules prerender.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus feature ID 5145343574409216 (Service Worker support for speculation rules prefetch) instead of the principal feature ID 6247959677108224 (Sec-Purpose on link rel=prefetch).

design_system.rationale: The styles use the shared CSS variables from the design system correctly and maintain good contrast.

openQuestions.title: Correct ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The ChromeStatus link on the header-trace page points to feature ID 5145343574409216 (Service Worker support for speculation rules prefetch) rather than the parent feature ID 6247959677108224 (Sec-Purpose header with <link rel=prefetch>). Correct this reference.

summary: An excellent header tracer contrasting multiple prefetch mechanisms with a nice compatibility matrix, though it links to the wrong parent ChromeStatus entry.
